name: 深圳
description: Shenzhen is China's first Special Economic Zone and a global tech hub. A modern megacity that transformed from a fishing village to one of the world's fastest-growing cities in just 40 years. Home to tech giants like Tencent, Huawei, and BYD. Features innovative architecture, theme parks, and easy access to Hong Kong.
highlights: Window of the World - Global landmarks

climate.tips: Best visited in winter (Nov-Feb) when weather is mild and pleasant. Summer is hot, humid with frequent typhoons. Spring is rainy. Always bring sunscreen and hydration.

attractions.name: 世界之窗
attractions.description: Theme park featuring miniature replicas of famous world landmarks including Eiffel Tower, pyramids, and Taj Mahal.
attractions.tips: Start early to cover all attractions. Evening is best for photos. Book tickets online for discount.

attractions.name: 华侨城创意园
attractions.description: Former industrial complex converted into vibrant arts and design hub with galleries, boutiques, cafes, and creative studios.
attractions.address: North Area of OCT, Nanshan District
attractions.tips: Best on weekends for markets and events. The area transforms during Chinese New Year with art exhibitions.

attractions.name: 大芬油画村
attractions.description: The world's largest oil painting production base, where millions of replicas of famous artworks are painted. Now also an art destination.
attractions.tips: Bargaining expected when buying paintings. Quality varies widely. Commission paintings on-site at reasonable prices.

attractions.name: 深圳湾公园
attractions.description: Coastal park along Shenzhen Bay with scenic waterfront, mangroves, bird watching, and Hong Kong skyline views.
attractions.tips: Best at sunset for Hong Kong skyline photos. Morning for bird watching. Bring binoculars.

attractions.name: 海上世界
attractions.description: Italian-themed waterfront complex centered on a historic Italian cruise ship, featuring restaurants, bars, and entertainment.
attractions.tips: Evening visit recommended for atmosphere. Great for seafood restaurants. Take ferry from Hong Kong for scenic arrival.

attractions.name: 欢乐谷
attractions.description: Large amusement park with roller coasters, water rides, and performances for all ages.
attractions.tips: Weekday visits avoid crowds. Height restrictions on rides. Re-entry allowed with hand stamp.

attractions.name: 平安金融中心
attractions.description: The second tallest building in China with observation deck offering panoramic city views from 542 meters.
attractions.tips: Book tickets online to avoid queues. Go at sunset for day and night views. Avoid foggy days.

attractions.name: 海岸城
attractions.description: Large waterfront shopping complex with international brands, restaurants, and entertainment in Nanshan.
attractions.tips: Good for rainy days. Connected to Metro. Excellent restaurants on upper floors.

restaurants.name: 利苑酒家
restaurants.address: 2F, Shenzhen Times Square, Luobao Road, Luohu
restaurants.description: Two Michelin-starred Cantonese fine dining with exceptional dim sum and premium seafood.

restaurants.name: 粤菜王府
restaurants.address: 2F, Park 'N Shop Center, Jintian Road, Futian
restaurants.description: Two-diamond Black Pearl restaurant specializing in authentic Cantonese cuisine with elegant setting.

restaurants.name: 润园四季椰子鸡
restaurants.nameEn: Run Yuan Si Ji Coconut Chicken
restaurants.description: Famous for tender coconut chicken hot pot, a favorite among locals for healthy and delicious dining.

restaurants.name: 金稻园砂锅粥
restaurants.description: Popular late-night spot for thick Cantonese congee and fresh seafood dishes.

restaurants.name: 华强北小吃街
restaurants.description: Bustling street food area near electronics market with variety of local snacks and quick bites.

restaurants.name: 蔡澜点心
restaurants.description: Michelin-starred dim sum by celebrity chef Chua Lam. Modern presentation with traditional flavors.

restaurants.name: 日日香鹅肉馆
restaurants.description: Famous for salt-baked goose and traditional Chaozhou dishes. Local favorite with honest pricing.

restaurants.name: 探鱼
restaurants.description: Popular chain known for creatively grilled fish dishes with Sichuan spices and local flavors.

arrival.duration: Varies by origin (3h from Hong Kong, 12h from Europe)
arrival.frequency: Daily flights from Asia, Europe, Americas
arrival.tips: Airport is 35km from city center. Metro Line 11 to downtown. Airport shuttle bus available.

arrival.to: Shenzhen Railway Station / Shenzhen North Station / Shenzhen East
arrival.tips: High-speed rail from Hong Kong West Kowloon is very convenient. Futian Station is central.

metro: Single ride: 2-10 CNY depending on distance
bus: Extensive network covering areas not served by metro
Many routes run until late night
taxi: Didi works well for English users
bike: Unlock: 1 CNY, then 0.5-1 CNY per 30 minutes
Designated bike lanes on many roads
Shared bikes forbidden on some roads

hotels.name: 深圳华侨城洲际酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Weekend packages good value. Check for OCT discounts.

hotels.name: 深圳瑞吉酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Club rooms worth upgrade for privileges. Book direct for benefits.

hotels.name: 深圳香格里拉大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for those entering from Hong Kong. Good for transit.

hotels.name: 深圳格兰云天酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Check weekend rates. Book early during conventions.

hotels.name: 深圳东门酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Great for shopping tourists. Basic but clean.

culturalTips.title: Tech Culture
culturalTips.content: Shenzhen is China's tech hub. Visit Huaqiangbei electronics market for gadgets. Experience cashless society firsthand.

culturalTips.title: Fast Pace
culturalTips.content: Shenzhen is always moving. Metro is packed during rush hours (7:30-9:00, 17:30-19:30). Plan accordingly.

culturalTips.title: Dining Hours
culturalTips.content: Local restaurants busy during normal meal times: 11:30-13:30 and 17:30-20:30. Late-night dining common.

culturalTips.title: Language
culturalTips.content: Mandarin is primary. Cantonese widely spoken in Luohu and traditional areas. English more common in tourist areas.

emergencyContacts.name: 美国驻广州总领事馆
emergencyContacts.notes: For US citizens in Shenzhen region

quickFacts.visa: Most nationalities require visa; Hong Kong visit if eligible
quickFacts.water: Tap water safe; bottled water widely available
